reviews.review_description: Good for VR beginners, and in later levels gets really difficult and exciting even for VR pros.

Really fun game and also a decent workout. My favorite thing and probably most unique is the iOS companion where you can throw things at someone else (or yourself) playing on VR. Would love to see more content - the level list has a lot of room for more.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review! I'm glad to hear you are enjoying Party Mode. Regarding content, I recently added 2 new campaigns with a lot of new levels specifically for the new Mixed Reality update which you might also enjoy.

reviews.review_description: Practising dodging stuff has become a favourite pastime of mine of late, so had to pick this up.  And it's a lot of fun.  Plenty of different things to dodge in different ways.  And the 360 dodging from multiple angles, makes it one of the more challenging dodge games I've played.  Well worth a play.  One thing though (and I think I read that this was coming a little while back), it really, REALLY, would benefit from mixed reality.  Currently I have to play it quite tentatively.  MR would be a MASSIVE upgrade here.  GG though. =)

developer_response.body: Thanks for the honest review! I just released Mixed Reality update in December, so please give it a try and let me know what you think.

reviews.review_description:
This VR game brings a refreshing blend of fast reflex gameplay and story-driven action. You’ll find yourself dodging an onslaught of objects with different shapes and speeds, all while uncovering a mysterious world connected by portals and enemies. What really makes it special is how failure doesn’t feel frustrating—it’s actually fun to get hit sometimes, because each level is such a ride.

The main character’s superpowers—blocking, destroying, or slowing down obstacles—add a strategic layer to the experience. It’s not just about quick reflexes, but also timing and choice. With its immersive storytelling, clever mechanics, and a nice dose of challenge, this game is definitely worth a shot for fans of action and adventure in VR.

reviews.review_title: Great idea, hope there's more coming
reviews.review_description:
This is a great start. I've only played the first few levels of the pirate's campaign, all of which have been pretty easy. Not quite sure how to get a full 3 stars, though...I didn't come close to getting hit and still only earned two stars on a few levels.

It is worth noting that using the right stick to select and toggle between them is pretty clunky. I hope that mechanic is improved in the future.

Also, why are the treasure chests thrown so high? They never come close to me, even if I'm standing still. Is this intentional design?

I'd love to see some sleeker graphics. The cartoonish theme and objects are cute for a short minute but I think I'd get into the game more if it was a bit darker or and more ethereal or abstract. More campaigns would be great. There doesn't seem to be a lot of content at the moment, but assuming the devs continue to update moving forward (which it seems they will), this could be quite a gem.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your honest review! I added 2 new campaigns with more levels for the new Mixed Reality update which might be interesting to you. I also updated some of the initial levels to be more challenging. The stars are received for how well you CLOSE you are to getting hit - it's a "style" rating, so when you dodge dangerously, you get more stars :)

The treasure chests are intentionally above your head, but when they are above you then they tip down to attack you (to prevent players from lying on the floor). There was an issue in the version you were playing where they didn't always attack, but that is fixed now.

reviews.review_description: The game is designed very well and is pretty challenging.  You will need a large space as you will be moving a lot and in different directions. The tutorial is very helpful and will get you started very quickly. Lots of different modes to keep things interesting. No mixed reality but it says it's coming in the game. I'll need that as I don't have too much room and seeing my surroundings will help. Very happy with this game and will use it a lot.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your lovely review! Mixed Reality update is now live, with all new levels as well as Party Mode support, help you avoid crashing into your furniture :)

reviews.review_title: Fun with a workout side benefit
reviews.review_description:
When I first started the game I was frustrated and not dodging properly.  Going through the tutorial was the key for me to understand all of the controls and how much actually I needed to move (having lots of space makes this very fun!!) 
Once I made it through the tutorial and started the campaign, I loved it. The first few levels give you the feel for it and then everything starts moving quickly and it gets intense. It’s the first game in which I yelled ‘ouch’ when I got hit and had family members come to see if I was okay. It did glitch once or twice early on where my hands were floating and there was a restart needed, but it’s new so that will be ironed out quickly. Over all, really impressed with this and I’m looking forward to playing it often. Enjoyed by all aged 12 to 50 in this household

developer_response.body:
That's hilarious about yelling 'ouch' - and wonderful that you were so immersed!

I am working on the floating hands issue, thank you for letting me know that it's still happening as it only affects some players. It can usually be fixed by looking at your hands and moving your controllers around, but I will make this a top priority in the coming days.

reviews.review_description:
I’ve played a couple of times and it’s ok, seems pretty slow at the start. 
Due to space restrictions I prefer games where I don’t have to move my feet too much & I can see that this game is going to need a bit more space.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your honest review! I adjusted the starting levels to be a bit more challenging. If you look in Bonus Levels, there are a lot of new challenges which are much faster if you wish to get right into it without having to unlock levels in Campaign Mode. On top of that, after you unlock a level in Campaign Mode, you can play it again in Endless Mode, where each iteration is faster - you play until it's too fast to play anymore.

The Mixed Reality update was also just released to help move around tight spaces easier.

reviews.review_description:
Woah.. this gets intense fast! 

Starts off nice and slow and makes you think yeah, I’ve got this… I’m goooooood!!!

But before you know it, stuff is flying at you from every angle! 

It gets you turning, twisting, ducking and dodging at all angles.

Graphics and sound are great!
But be warned… you need a lot of space for this… outside on a dry day would be ideal.

If you like your fitness about moving and dodging, while you burn off calories, then this is for you.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your lovely and energetic review!

A big play area is indeed nice, but DodgeALL is also designed to be played in smaller areas as well (my apartment is very small). I recently added scaling to obstacles based on your play area size, as well as Mixed Reality support, so you can see your room while you dodge things.

reviews.review_description:
It’s always pleasant to find a new hidden gem
Dodge all is one of those types of games.

Clear your play space and get ready for something different.
Starts off very easy, I’m thinking I’m king of the high seas, before long I’m bending and twisting in all directions.
Price is very fair, if you want a different type of fitness game then add this to your collection.
Well done devs.

DodgeAll is a great game to get you supple from so much bending and dodging, leaning to have those sacks, swords and batons miss you thrown at you by pesky Pirates.
The game’s variety of challenges and levels keeps it fresh and exciting, while the graphics are simplistic they are really clear and engaging.  I would appreciate a sound track to dodge and weave to, maybe I missed it in settings or it will be updated.  
The pace is good in game but boy it got fast and hard as I progressed through a few levels.  This will give you a work out as you start hitting the harder levels and things get thrown at you more often making it harder to dodge.

This is a great early access start and I look forward to seeing where the dev will take it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your great feedback! Music is currently in the works and will be released soon.

reviews.review_description: slightly crazy de locos. funny references and colorful visuals

Well…  I wanted something as fitness, workout help, not to become ninja. 😊

Slow on start, and I didn’t get all possible controls what you could do. But for me it’s fun and I like it.

You are getting stuff from every direction, and it gets intense pretty quic.

I’m not dancing as with BeatSaber, but is another kind of workout. Ha ha ha

Worth a shot if you’re looking for something active and addictive!


DodgeALL 👍🏼

double-check guardian area before start, (did I said that already?
Especially when the start throwing objects in all different directions.  You have to avoid Fruit, rocks, treasures, big bones and many other stud coming at you.  Very fun and good workout
This is a really interesting full room game! I havn't seen to many games like this. It starts off nice and slow then gets crazy! I'd recomend it!
This game is irreverent and fun!  It involves a lot of physical activity too, it really keeps you moving.  Good work out, and fun!
